speaker: RICHARD
text: Good day, my lord. What, at your book so hard?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ay, my good lord—“my lord,” I should say rather.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: ’Tis sin to flatter; “good” was little better:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: “Good Gloucester” and “good devil” were alike,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And both preposterous: therefore, not “good lord.”

speaker: RICHARD
text: Sirrah, leave us to ourselves; we must confer.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So flies the reckless shepherd from the wolf;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So first the harmless sheep doth yield his fleece

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And next his throat unto the butcher’s knife.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What scene of death hath Roscius now to act?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Suspicion always haunts the guilty mind;

speaker: RICHARD
text: The thief doth fear each bush an officer.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The bird that hath been limèd in a bush,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With trembling wings misdoubteth every bush;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And I, the hapless male to one sweet bird,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Have now the fatal object in my eye

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Where my poor young was limed, was caught, and killed.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why, what a peevish fool was that of Crete

speaker: RICHARD
text: That taught his son the office of a fowl!

speaker: RICHARD
text: And yet, for all his wings, the fool was drowned.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I Daedalus, my poor boy Icarus,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thy father Minos, that denied our course;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The sun that seared the wings of my sweet boy

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thy brother Edward, and thyself the sea

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whose envious gulf did swallow up his life.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ah, kill me with thy weapon, not with words!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My breast can better brook thy dagger’s point

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Than can my ears that tragic history.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But wherefore dost thou come? Is ’t for my life?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Think’st thou I am an executioner?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: A persecutor I am sure thou art.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If murdering innocents be executing,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, then, thou art an executioner.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thy son I killed for his presumption.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Hadst thou been killed when first thou didst presume,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thou hadst not lived to kill a son of mine.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And thus I prophesy: that many a thousand

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Which now mistrust no parcel of my fear,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And many an old man’s sigh, and many a widow’s

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And many an orphan’s water-standing eye,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Men for their sons, wives for their husbands,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Orphans for their parents’ timeless death,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Shall rue the hour that ever thou wast born.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The owl shrieked at thy birth, an evil sign;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The night-crow cried, aboding luckless time;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Dogs howled, and hideous tempest shook down trees;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The raven rooked her on the chimney’s top;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And chatt’ring pies in dismal discords sung;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thy mother felt more than a mother’s pain,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And yet brought forth less than a mother’s hope:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To wit, an indigested and deformèd lump,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Not like the fruit of such a goodly tree.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Teeth hadst thou in thy head when thou wast born

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To signify thou cam’st to bite the world.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And if the rest be true which I have heard,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thou cam’st—

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll hear no more. Die, prophet, in thy speech;

speaker: RICHARD
text: For this amongst the rest was I ordained.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ay, and for much more slaughter after this.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O God, forgive my sins, and pardon thee.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What, will the aspiring blood of Lancaster

speaker: RICHARD
text: Sink in the ground? I thought it would have mounted.

speaker: RICHARD
text: See how my sword weeps for the poor king’s death.

speaker: RICHARD
text: O, may such purple tears be always shed

speaker: RICHARD
text: From those that wish the downfall of our house.

speaker: RICHARD
text: If any spark of life be yet remaining,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Down, down to hell, and say I sent thee thither—

speaker: RICHARD
text: I that have neither pity, love, nor fear.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Indeed, ’tis true that Henry told me of,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For I have often heard my mother say

speaker: RICHARD
text: I came into the world with my legs forward.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Had I not reason, think you, to make haste

speaker: RICHARD
text: And seek their ruin that usurped our right?

speaker: RICHARD
text: The midwife wondered, and the women cried

speaker: RICHARD
text: “O Jesus bless us, he is born with teeth!”

speaker: RICHARD
text: And so I was, which plainly signified

speaker: RICHARD
text: That I should snarl, and bite, and play the dog.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then, since the heavens have shaped my body so,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Let hell make crook’d my mind to answer it.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I have no brother, I am like no brother;

speaker: RICHARD
text: And this word “love,” which graybeards call divine,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Be resident in men like one another

speaker: RICHARD
text: And not in me. I am myself alone.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Clarence, beware; thou keep’st me from the light,

speaker: RICHARD
text: But I will sort a pitchy day for thee;

speaker: RICHARD
text: For I will buzz abroad such prophecies

speaker: RICHARD
text: That Edward shall be fearful of his life;

speaker: RICHARD
text: And then to purge his fear, I’ll be thy death.

speaker: RICHARD
text: King Henry and the Prince his son are gone.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Clarence, thy turn is next, and then the rest,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Counting myself but bad till I be best.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll throw thy body in another room,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And triumph, Henry, in thy day of doom.
